summary: Successfully synthesized comprehensive design principles document from all Stream 1 research. Created 7 core principles for V's ADHD-optimized task system: Hide the Wall/Show the Path, Body Doubling as Infrastructure, Immediate Task Breakdown, Shame-Proof Accountability, Energy-Aware Design, Arsenal FC Identity Layer, and Graduation by Design. Document includes specific feature recommendations, nag mode implementation strategy, accountability partner model, gamification guidance, and phased implementation roadmap.
core_principles: Hide the Wall, Show the Path - focus on next action, never overwhelming backlogs
Body Doubling as Core Infrastructure - virtual co-presence and accountability partnerships
Immediate Task Breakdown - AI-powered decomposition integrated into workflow
Shame-Proof Accountability - guilt-based messaging, never shame-based
Energy-Aware System Design - adapt to ADHD's variable capacity
Arsenal FC Identity Layer - leverage existing passion for engagement
Graduation by Design - build toward independence, not dependency
must_have_features: AI-powered task breakdown with first-step suggestions
Virtual body doubling and shared task visibility
Progress indicators with immediate dopamine feedback
Shame-proof language patterns and escape hatches
Arsenal FC theming with football metaphor celebrations
Graduated nag mode with safeguards and overrides
avoid_features: Prominent task counts or overdue lists
Complex feature sets requiring extensive configuration
notes_for_orchestrator: This synthesis represents the culmination of comprehensive ADHD research and should be V's primary reference for building the system. Key insight: This isn't just another to-do app—it's a chief of staff system that works with ADHD brain patterns. The phased implementation approach prioritizes foundation → accountability → intelligence → integration. Arsenal FC theming is strongly supported by research but should be simple visual identity, not mechanical complexity. The document is comprehensive (6000+ words) but structured for digestibility with clear sections V can reference during development.
